compiled_state.title: inbound-bridge: Jira-created bugs not synced to local ticket system -> DIG-1546, DIG-1547, DIG-1548 have no local tickets
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
Three bugs were created directly in Jira (DIG-1546, DIG-1547, DIG-1548) but have no corresponding local tickets. The Inbound Bridge runs on a schedule and should create local tickets for new Jira issues, but these 3 bugs (all type Bug, status To Do) were never pulled in.

## Expected Behavior
The Inbound Bridge should detect new Jira issues that have no local counterpart and create corresponding local tickets with matching type/status.

## Actual Behavior
DIG-1546 ('Jira is not updated with in progress epics, stories, or tasks'), DIG-1547 ('DIG-1470 is still shown as in progress in Jira'), and DIG-1548 ('Jira failures are not surfaced to the user when bridge operations fail') exist only in Jira with no local representation. Searched 3247 local tickets (including archived) — zero matches by bridge_key or title.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
The Inbound Bridge may only sync issues that were originally created by the Outbound Bridge (i.e., issues with a known bridge_key mapping). Issues created directly in Jira without a prior outbound sync may be ignored.

comments.body:
Relevant ticket details:

JIRA ISSUES WITH NO LOCAL COUNTERPART:
- DIG-1546 (Bug/To Do): 'Jira is not updated with in progress epics, stories, or tasks'
  - Description: null (no description body in Jira)
  - Assignee: null
  - Created in Jira, never synced inbound
- DIG-1547 (Bug/To Do): 'DIG-1470 is still shown as in progress in Jira'
  - Description: null
  - Assignee: null
  - Created in Jira, never synced inbound
- DIG-1548 (Bug/To Do): 'Jira failures are not surfaced to the user when bridge operations fail'
  - Description: null
  - Assignee: null
  - Created in Jira, never synced inbound

LOCAL SEARCH (3247 tickets including archived): zero matches by bridge_key reference or title substring for any of these 3 Jira keys.

RELATED: The Inbound Bridge had 3 push failures in the last week (bug d411-c0e7) due to concurrent tickets branch updates. However, these 3 Jira bugs likely predate those failures — they appear to have never been candidates for inbound sync at all.
comments.author: Test
